summarization.a: X-ray diffraction patterns of Fe<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub> samples grown at 425°C.
summarization.b: X-ray diffraction patterns of Fe<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub>:MgO samples grown at 425°C with Mg/Fe = 0.02.
summarization.c: X-ray diffraction patterns of Fe<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub>:MgO samples grown at 425°C with Mg/Fe = 0.23.

c.question: Does the addition of magnesium have an affect on crystallinity after the FGA?
c.answer: It seems that for all three cases the final peaks are similar after the FGA, this suggests that magnesium does not have a huge impact.

c.question: What is the supercycle rate of magnesium and iron oxide?

a.question: Does a functional gas anneal have an influence on the crystallinity of iron oxide? If so, what effect?
a.answer: a	The FGA shifts the hematite phase to the magnetite phase.

b.question: What could be a reason for the initial 100 cycles of iron oxide before the supercycle process is started?
b.answer: It could be that magnesium oxide does not grow that good on a different substrate than on iron oxide. By first depositing iron oxide, nucleation delays can be suppressed.
